title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Lamb
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi with our whole slow roasted recipe. Perfect for a feast, this tender lamb dish is a showstopper. Get the recipe!
longTailKeywords: authentic Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi recipe
lowCompetitionKeywords: slow roasted whole lamb ouzi at home
seo.h1: Authentic Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Perfection
h2Headings: Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
Step-by-Step: How to Make Lamb Ouzi
Expert Tips for the Best Lamb Ouzi
Serving Suggestions for Your Ouzi Feast
seo.imageAlt: A beautifully presented whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi, a traditional Middle Eastern dish with tender lamb and aromatic rice.
seo.imageCaption: Impress your guests with this stunning whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi, a centerpiece for any Middle Eastern gathering!

description: Experience the true taste of Middle Eastern hospitality with this stunning Lamb Ouzi. A whole lamb shoulder or leg is slow roasted to fall-off-the-bone tenderness, infused with aromatic spices. Served majestically over a bed of fragrant spiced rice, this dish is a celebration of flavors perfect for any grand occasion.

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb Marinade
instructions.text: In a bowl, combine minced garlic, olive oil, 2 tablespoons Baharat, cumin, coriander, 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon, 1/2 teaspoon cardamom, salt, pepper, and lemon juice. Mix well to form a thick paste.

instructions.title: Marinate and Prepare for Roasting
instructions.text: Pat the lamb shoulder or leg dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make several deep incisions all over the lamb. Rub the marinade generously into the lamb, ensuring it gets into all the incisions. Place the lamb in a large roasting pan.

instructions.title: Slow Roast the Lamb
instructions.text: Add 1/2 cup water or broth to the bottom of the roasting pan. Cover the pan tightly with aluminum foil. Preheat oven to 160C (325F). Roast the lamb for 3 to 3.5 hours, or until it is incredibly fork-tender and easily pulls apart. Remove the foil for the last 30 minutes of cooking to allow the lamb to brown slightly. Once cooked, remove the lamb from the oven, tent it loosely with foil, and let it rest for 15-20 minutes.

instructions.title: Prepare the Spiced Rice
instructions.text: While the lamb is resting, heat ghee or vegetable oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until it is softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in 1 tablespoon Baharat, 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon, and 1/4 teaspoon cardamom, and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Cook the Rice
instructions.text: Add the soaked and drained basmati rice to the pot, stirring gently to coat the grains with the spices and onion. Pour in the hot lamb or chicken broth and add salt to taste. Bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce the heat to low, stir in the frozen peas and carrots, cover the pot tightly, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender.

instructions.title: Assemble and Garnish
instructions.text: Fluff the cooked spiced rice with a fork. Spread the rice evenly onto a large serving platter. Carefully place the whole slow roasted lamb on top of the bed of rice. Garnish generously with the toasted slivered almonds, toasted pine nuts, and fresh chopped parsley. Serve immediately with a side of yogurt or a simple salad.

tips: For an even deeper flavor, marinate the lamb overnight in the refrigerator. This allows the spices to penetrate the meat thoroughly, resulting in a more aromatic and tender roast.
Do not skip resting the lamb after roasting. This crucial step allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, ensuring it remains succulent and tender when carved.
Toasting the nuts (almonds and pine nuts) before garnishing adds a wonderful crunch and enhances their nutty flavor, elevating the overall texture and taste of the Ouzi.
article:
Embark on a culinary adventure to the heart of the Middle East, where flavors are rich, aromas are intoxicating, and hospitality reigns supreme. Few dishes embody this spirit quite like Lamb Ouzi, a magnificent centerpiece often reserved for grand celebrations and cherished family gatherings. While Ouzi can take many forms, the "Whole Slow Roasted" version elevates it to an art form, transforming humble ingredients into a tender, aromatic feast that delights the senses. This article will guide you through mastering this impressive Middle Eastern dinner recipe, ensuring your next special occasion is truly unforgettable as you prepare to create the ultimate Lamb Ouzi recipe.

## What is Lamb Ouzi?

Lamb Ouzi (sometimes spelled "Uzi" or "Oozy") is a quintessential Middle Eastern dish, celebrated across the Levant and Gulf regions. At its core, Ouzi typically features slow-cooked, incredibly tender lamb or goat, often served over a bed of fragrant, spiced basmati rice, adorned with toasted nuts and sometimes green peas or minced meat. It’s a dish synonymous with generosity and festivity, embodying the warmth of Middle Eastern hospitality. While some Ouzi might involve individual pastries filled with lamb and rice, the grandest expression, and our focus today, is the whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi. This method involves patiently cooking a large cut of lamb, such as a shoulder or leg, until it’s fall-off-the-bone tender, allowing its rich flavors to meld beautifully with a symphony of spices. The result is a deeply savory, aromatic, and utterly satisfying meal that is as much a spectacle as it is a culinary delight.

## Ingredients for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

To create this impressive Lamb Ouzi recipe, you'll need quality ingredients that will build layers of flavor.

For the Whole Slow Roasted Lamb:
*   **Lamb:** A 5-7 lb (2.5-3.5 kg) whole lamb shoulder or leg, bone-in, is ideal for slow roasting.
*   **Olive Oil:** 1/4 cup, for rubbing.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** Generous amounts, to taste.
*   **Ouzi Spice Blend:** 2 tbsp ground allspice, 1 tbsp ground cardamom, 1 tsp ground cinnamon, 1/2 tsp ground nutmeg.
*   **Aromatics:** 2 large onions, quartered; 6-8 cloves garlic, smashed.
*   **Liquid:** 4 cups water or low-sodium lamb/chicken broth.
*   **Optional:** 2-3 bay leaves, a few sprigs of fresh rosemary or thyme.

For the Ouzi Spiced Rice:
*   **Basmati Rice:** 3 cups, rinsed thoroughly until water runs clear.
*   **Olive Oil or Ghee:** 2 tbsp.
*   **Onion:** 1 medium, finely chopped.
*   **Garlic:** 3 cloves, minced.
*   **Ouzi Spice Blend (for rice):** 1 tsp ground allspice, 1/2 tsp ground cardamom, 1/4 tsp ground cinnamon, pinch of ground nutmeg.
*   **Salt & Pepper:** To taste.
*   **Broth:** 4 cups (from the lamb roasting pan, if possible, or low-sodium chicken/vegetable broth).
*   **Green Peas:** 1 cup frozen, thawed.
*   **Optional:** 1/2 lb (225g) ground lamb or beef, cooked and crumbled.

For Garnish:
*   **Toasted Nuts:** 1/2 cup pine nuts, slivered almonds, or cashews, lightly toasted in a dry pan.
*   **Fresh Parsley or Mint:** 1/4 cup, chopped, for garnish.

## Step-by-Step: How to Make Lamb Ouzi

Following this detailed Lamb Ouzi recipe will guide you to a perfect result.

1.  **Prepare the Lamb:** Pat the lamb shoulder/leg dry with paper towels. Score the fatty side of the lamb deeply in a crosshatch pattern, being careful not to cut into the meat too much. In a small bowl, combine the olive oil, salt, pepper, and the lamb Ouzi spice blend. Rub this mixture generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into the scores. For best results, cover and refrigerate overnight to marinate. If short on time, marinate for at least 2 hours at room temperature.
2.  **Sear (Optional, but Recommended):** Preheat a large, heavy-bottomed oven-safe pot or roasting pan over medium-high heat. Sear the lamb on all sides until nicely browned. This step adds a wonderful depth of flavor and color.
3.  **Slow Roasting:** Place the seared (or unseared) lamb in a sturdy roasting pan. Scatter the quartered onions, smashed garlic, and bay leaves around the lamb. Pour the 4 cups of water or broth into the bottom of the pan. Cover the pan tightly with foil or a lid.
4.  **Roast:** Preheat your oven to a low temperature, around 300°F (150°C). Roast the lamb for 4-6 hours, or until it is incredibly tender and practically falling off the bone. The exact time will depend on the size of your lamb and your oven. Halfway through cooking, you can baste the lamb with the pan juices.
5.  **Rest the Lamb:** Once cooked, carefully remove the lamb from the oven. Transfer it to a cutting board, tent loosely with foil, and let it rest for at least 20-30 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in a more tender and flavorful meat. While it rests, strain the pan juices and reserve for the rice or as a serving sauce.
6.  **Prepare the Ouzi Spiced Rice:** While the lamb is resting, prepare the rice. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil or ghee over medium heat. Sauté the chopped onion until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
7.  **Add Spices and Rice:** Stir in the Ouzi spice blend for the rice (allspice, cardamom, cinnamon, nutmeg), salt, and pepper. Cook for 1 minute, allowing the spices to bloom. Add the rinsed basmati rice and stir to coat every grain with the spiced oil. If using ground meat, add it now and cook until browned before adding the spices and rice.
8.  **Cook the Rice:** Pour in the 4 cups of reserved lamb broth (or fresh broth). Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender. Do not lift the lid during cooking.
9.  **Finish the Rice:** Once cooked, turn off the heat and let the rice stand, covered, for another 10 minutes. Gently fluff with a fork and stir in the thawed green peas.
10. **Assemble and Serve:** On a large serving platter, spread the fluffy Ouzi spiced rice. Carefully transfer the whole slow-roasted lamb onto the bed of rice. You can shred the lamb directly onto the platter, or serve it whole for a more dramatic presentation, letting guests pull off pieces. Garnish generously with toasted nuts and fresh chopped parsley or mint.

## Expert Tips for the Best Lamb Ouzi

Mastering the perfect Lamb Ouzi recipe takes patience and a few expert tricks.

*   **Quality Lamb is Key:** Start with a good quality, bone-in lamb shoulder or leg. The bone adds flavor and helps keep the meat moist.
*   **Marination is Your Friend:** Don't skip the marination step. Overnight marination allows the spices to penetrate deeply, infusing the lamb with incredible flavor.
*   **Low and Slow is the Way:** The secret to truly tender, fall-off-the-bone lamb is slow roasting at a low temperature for an extended period. This breaks down connective tissues, making the meat incredibly succulent.
*   **Don't Forget to Rest:** After roasting, resting the lamb is crucial. It allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, resulting in a more tender and juicy final product.
*   **Flavorful Broth:** Using the strained pan juices from the lamb to cook the rice adds an extra layer of richness and depth to the Ouzi rice.
*   **Toast Your Nuts:** Lightly toasting the nuts (pine nuts, almonds) before garnishing enhances their flavor and adds a delightful crunch.
*   **Rinse Your Rice:** Rinsing basmati rice thoroughly removes excess starch, preventing it from becoming sticky and ensuring fluffy, separate grains.
*   **Taste and Adjust:** Always taste your spice blends and adjust to your preference. Some prefer more cardamom, others more allspice.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Ouzi Feast

Completing your Lamb Ouzi recipe with the right accompaniments elevates the entire meal into a true Middle Eastern feast.

*   **Yogurt Sauce (Laban):** A simple plain yogurt or a refreshing cucumber and mint yogurt sauce (like "khyar bi laban") provides a cooling contrast to the rich lamb and spiced rice.
*   **Simple Green Salad:** A fresh salad with crisp lettuce, tomatoes, cucumbers, and a light lemon-olive oil dressing cuts through the richness.
*   **Pickles (Torshi):** Assorted Middle Eastern pickled vegetables offer a tangy counterpoint.
*   **Fresh Bread:** Warm pita bread or other flatbreads are perfect for scooping up any remaining rice or lamb juices.
*   **Tomato Sauce:** Sometimes a simple, light tomato-based sauce, lightly spiced, is served on the side for those who prefer extra moisture or tang.
*   **Hummus or Baba Ghanoush:** These classic dips make excellent appetizers or side dishes.

## Variations & Substitutions for Lamb Ouzi

While this Lamb Ouzi recipe focuses on the whole slow roasted version, there are several ways to adapt it:

*   **Meat Alternatives:** While lamb is traditional, you can use goat for a similar flavor profile, or even a bone-in beef roast (like a chuck roast) if lamb is unavailable, though cooking times and flavor will vary slightly.
*   **Spice Adjustments:** Feel free to adjust the spice ratios to your liking. Some recipes include dried lime (loomi) for a tangy note, or a pinch of saffron for color and aroma in the rice.
*   **Rice Fillings:** Beyond green peas and ground meat, you can add diced carrots, chickpeas, or even a handful of golden raisins for a touch of sweetness to the rice.
*   **Cooking Method for Lamb:** If you're short on time, a pressure cooker can significantly reduce the cooking time for the lamb, though it won't achieve the same depth of roasted flavor and texture. For smaller cuts, you could braise the lamb on the stovetop.
*   **Nut Varieties:** Experiment with different toasted nuts like pistachios or a mix of several types for varied texture and flavor.

Whether for a grand celebration or a cherished family gathering, this Lamb Ouzi recipe promises an unforgettable dining experience. Its rich history, comforting flavors, and impressive presentation make it a dish that truly stands out. Enjoy the process of creating this Middle Eastern masterpiece, and savor every tender, aromatic bite.
